title: RequestExtracted
description: Published by IA1 (NLP Triage) after turning the patient's free text into structured data. Consumed by both IA2 (urgency scoring) and IA3 (matching) — so this shape is the shared 'understanding' of the request that the rest of the pipeline builds on.

event_id.description: Unique ID for this specific event instance (not the request).

request_id.description: Correlation ID shared by every event belonging to the same patient request, from submission to closure. This is how Evaluation reconstructs a full request lifecycle across services.

version.description: Schema version for this event type, e.g. '1.0'. Bump on breaking changes so consumers can branch on it during migration.

produced_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the producing service emitted this event.

producer.description: Name of the service that published this event, e.g. 'ingestion', 'nlp-triage'. Useful for debugging and for Evaluation's traceability.

raw_text.description: Echo of the original patient text, kept alongside the extraction for traceability and for IA3 embedding fallback.

symptoms.description: Discrete symptoms/complaints identified in the text, e.g. ['douleur thoracique', 'essoufflement'].

severity_keywords.description: Phrases signalling severity/urgency, used as input signal for IA2, e.g. ['depuis ce matin', 'intense'].

duration_hint.description: How long the patient reports the issue has been present, if stated, e.g. '3 jours'.

specialty_hint.description: Free-text guess at the kind of professional needed, e.g. 'cardiologue'. This is NOT an exact tag — IA3 matches it semantically against professional profiles rather than doing exact string matching.

location_hint.description: Location mentioned in the text, if any, e.g. a neighborhood or city.

age_hint.description: Patient age if mentioned or inferable from text.

extraction_confidence.description: IA1's confidence in this extraction. Low values may warrant fallback handling downstream.
